ado-status-update.prompt: Run ADO autopilot LIVE for both meeting-join and meeting-notes areas. Note the current time as the job start time. Steps: (1) cd Q:/src/hackathon/ado-autopilot/ (2) git fetch origin main && git reset --hard origin/main to get latest code (never use git pull -- it creates merge commits on diverged branches) (3) Validate configs exist and are valid JSON (4) python src/main.py update-status --config configs/meeting-join.json --auto-approve (5) python src/main.py update-status --config configs/meeting-notes.json --auto-approve. This will generate reports, write approved updates to ADO, and upload reports to SharePoint AAP Reports folder automatically. The share URL is saved to reports/latest-share-url.txt for the poster agent to pick up.
ado-status-update.description: Runs ADO autopilot status tweet update (live) for Meeting Join and Meeting Notes areas

bug-autopilot-meeting-join.prompt: Run bug-autopilot LIVE for Meeting Join area bugs. Note the current time as the job start time. Steps: (1) cd Q:/src/hackathon/bug-autopilot/ (2) git pull to get latest code (3) Validate configs exist and are valid JSON (4) pip install -r requirements.txt --quiet (5) Run: powershell.exe -File bugautopilot.ps1 -Once.
bug-autopilot-meeting-join.description: Runs bug-autopilot live for Meeting Join bugs

ado-burndown-update.prompt: Run ADO autopilot burndown LIVE for both meeting-join and meeting-notes areas. Note the current time as the job start time. Steps: (1) cd Q:/src/hackathon/ado-autopilot/ (2) git fetch origin main && git reset --hard origin/main to get latest code (never use git pull -- it creates merge commits on diverged branches) (3) Validate configs exist and are valid JSON (4) python src/main.py update-burndown --config configs/meeting-join.json --auto-approve (5) python src/main.py update-burndown --config configs/meeting-notes.json --auto-approve. This will generate reports AND write approved burndown updates to ADO.
ado-burndown-update.description: Runs ADO autopilot burndown update (live) for Meeting Join and Meeting Notes areas

bug-autopilot-notes.prompt: Run bug-autopilot LIVE for Meeting Notes area bugs. Note the current time as the job start time. Steps: (1) cd Q:/src/hackathon/bug-autopilot/ (2) git pull to get latest code (3) Validate configs exist and are valid JSON (4) pip install -r requirements.txt --quiet (5) Run: powershell.exe -File bugautopilot.ps1 -Config Q:/src/hackathon/bug-autopilot/configs/meeting-notes.json -Once.
bug-autopilot-notes.description: Runs bug-autopilot live for Meeting Notes bugs

shiproom-hygiene-check.prompt: Run shiproom hygiene checks for Meeting Join and Meeting Notes areas. Note the current time as the job start time. This job runs a series of hygiene checks on ADO work items and fixes violations automatically. ## Check 1: Task-to-Task Parent Links Find all Tasks that have a parent relationship where the parent is also a Task (Tasks should be parented under User Stories or Features, never under other Tasks). Steps: 1. Get an ADO token: `az account get-access-token --resource 499b84ac-1321-427f-aa17-267ca6975798 --query accessToken -o tsv` 2. Run a WIQL link query to find Task-to-Task parent relationships in BOTH areas: - `MSTeams\Calling Meeting Devices (CMD)\Meetings\Meeting Join\Fundamentals` - `MSTeams\Calling Meeting Devices (CMD)\Meetings\Notes` Use this WIQL pattern: ``` SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.WorkItemType] FROM workitemLinks WHERE ([Source].[System.WorkItemType] = 'Task' AND [Source].[System.AreaPath] UNDER '') AND ([System.Links.LinkType] = 'System.LinkTypes.Hierarchy-Reverse') AND ([Target].[System.WorkItemType] = 'Task') MODE (MustContain) ``` 3. For each Task-to-Task parent pair found: a. GET the child work item with `$expand=relations` to find the parent relation index b. PATCH the child work item to remove the parent link (op: remove, path: /relations/{idx}) c. PATCH the child work item to add a Related link to the former parent (op: add, rel: System.LinkTypes.Related) d. Resolve the owner's identity ID via: `https://domoreexp.vssps.visualstudio.com/_apis/identities?searchFilter=General&filterValue={email}` e. Add a comment @mentioning the owner using the ADO comments API (`_apis/wit/workItems/{id}/comments`): Use HTML format with `@{name}` for the mention. Comment text: '{mention} FYI - The parent link on this task was changed to a Related link. Tasks should not be parented under another Task; use a User Story or Feature as the parent instead.' 4. If no violations found, log 'Check 1 passed: No Task-to-Task parent links found.' 5. If violations were fixed, log summary: 'Check 1: Fixed {N} Task-to-Task parent links (changed to Related).' ## Check 2: Close Resolved Work Items Find all work items in state 'Resolved' (any type) in both areas and close them. Steps: 1. Run a WIQL query to find all Resolved work items in BOTH areas: ``` SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.WorkItemType], [System.AssignedTo] FROM workitems WHERE [System.State] = 'Resolved' AND [System.AreaPath] UNDER '' ORDER BY [System.Id] ``` Run for both: - `MSTeams\Calling Meeting Devices (CMD)\Meetings\Meeting Join\Fundamentals` - `MSTeams\Calling Meeting Devices (CMD)\Meetings\Notes` 2. For each Resolved work item: a. PATCH the work item to change state to 'Closed' using the ADO REST API: `[{"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.State", "value": "Closed"}]` b. Add a comment (no @mention needed): 'Item should be closed instead of left Resolved. If there are any questions or issues with closing, please reach out to Josh Xu (qitxu@microsoft.com).' 3. If no violations found, log 'Check 2 passed: No Resolved items found.' 4. If violations were fixed, log summary: 'Check 2: Closed {N} Resolved work items.' ## Check 3: Move Non-Closed Items from Previous Sprint All items in the previous (completed) sprint should be Closed. Any non-closed items must be moved to the current sprint. Steps: 1. Get the current iteration for team CMD - Meeting Join (US) (team ID: 6f72ea4e-c73a-4a15-b622-46cdacc53987) using the iterations API with timeframe=current. 2. Get ALL iterations for the team and find the one immediately before the current one (by finishDate) -- this is the previous sprint. 3. Run a WIQL query to find all non-closed, non-removed items in the PREVIOUS iteration for BOTH areas: ``` SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.WorkItemType], [System.State] FROM workitems WHERE [System.State] <> 'Closed' AND [System.State] <> 'Removed' AND [System.IterationPath] = '' AND [System.AreaPath] UNDER '' ORDER BY [System.Id] ``` 4. For each item found, use the ADO REST PATCH API to change its iteration path to the current sprint: `[{"op": "add", "path": "/fields/System.IterationPath", "value": ""}]` 5. Add a comment to each moved item: 'This item was automatically moved from to because it was not closed when the sprint ended. If this item is no longer needed, please close it.' 6. If no items found, log 'Check 3 passed: No non-closed items in previous sprint.' 7. If items were moved, log summary: 'Check 3: Moved {N} items from to .' ## Output Write a summary to stdout listing: - Total checks run - Per-check: pass/fail, count of violations found and fixed - List of affected work item IDs with their owners
shiproom-hygiene-check.description: Shiproom hygiene checks: fixes Task-to-Task parent links, auto-closes Resolved items, and moves leftover items from previous sprint to current sprint
